questions.question: The standard equation of the parabola with focus at (a, 0) and directrix x = -a is ______.
The vertex of the parabola y² = 4ax is at ______.
The focus of the parabola y² = 4ax is at ______.
The diagram show a parabola opening rightward. Its axis of symmetry is the ______.
questions.diagram_description: A rightward-opening parabola with its axis of symmetry marked as the x-axis.

questions.question: The directrix of the parabola y² = 4ax is ______.
The latus rectum of the parabola y² = 4ax is the chord through the focus ______ to the directrix.
Length of the latus rectum of y² = 4ax is ______.
The diagram show the focal distance of point P on y²=4ax. The focal distance is ______.
questions.diagram_description: A parabola with a horizontal line (directrix) and lines showing the distance from P to the focus and to the directrix.

questions.question: The parametric equations of y² = 4ax are ______.

options.D: x = a cos t, y = a sin t

questions.question: Equation of the tangent at (at², 2at) on y² = 4ax is ______.
The condition for the line y = mx + c to be a tangent to y² = 4ax is ______.
The diagram show a normal to the parabola. It cuts the axis at G. The subnormal NG is always equal to ______.
questions.diagram_description: A parabola with a normal line from point P meeting the axis at G, and a vertical segment from P to the axis labeled N and G.

questions.question: If two tangents are drawn at t₁ and t₂ on y²=4ax, they meet at ______.
For the parabola y² = 4ax, the chord PQ where P = (at₁², 2at₁) and Q = (at₂², 2at₂) passes through the focus if ______.
The diagram show a focal chord of parabola y²=4ax. The locus of the midpoints of all focal chords is ______.
questions.diagram_description: Multiple focal chords drawn from focus, with their midpoints connected by a curve.

questions.question: Polar of a point (x₁, y₁) w.r.t. y² = 4ax is ______.
The tangent at the vertex of the parabola y²=4ax is the ______.
The diagram show a parabola x²=4ay opening upward. Its focus is at ______.
questions.diagram_description: An upward-opening parabola with its focus marked above the vertex.

questions.question: The eccentricity of a parabola is ______.
Equation of normal at (at², 2at) on y² = 4ax is ______.
If three normals are drawn from a point to a parabola, their slopes satisfy a ______ equation.
The chord of contact from (x₁, y₁) to y² = 4ax is ______.
The diagram show two tangents from an external point P to a parabola. They are always ______ to each other.
questions.diagram_description: Point P outside parabola y²=4ax with two tangent lines touching the curve.

options.A: Neither parallel nor perpendicular in general

questions.question: The pair of tangents from (x₁, y₁) to y² = 4ax is given by SS₁ = ______.
Tangents at the ends of a focal chord are ______ to each other.
The diagram show the latus rectum of y²=4ax as a chord. Its endpoints are ______.
questions.diagram_description: The parabola y²=4ax with the latus rectum as a vertical chord through the focus.

questions.question: The sum of the slopes of tangents from a point to y² = 4ax satisfies the condition m₁ + m₂ = ______.
If normals at P(t₁) and Q(t₂) meet at R(t₃) on y²=4ax, then t₁ + t₂ = ______.
The diagram show the axis of parabola y² = 4ax. It is the line ______.
questions.diagram_description: A parabola opening to the right with its central symmetry axis labeled.

questions.question: The locus of midpoints of chords parallel to y = mx for y²=4ax is ______.
The equation of the parabola with vertex at (h, k) and axis parallel to x-axis is ______.
The diagram show a reflective property: rays parallel to axis reflect through the ______.
questions.diagram_description: Parallel horizontal rays hitting the parabolic surface and all reflecting to converge at the focus.

questions.question: For y² = -4ax, the parabola opens in the ______ direction.
The tangent at the vertex of y²=4ax is perpendicular to the ______.
If the tangent at P(t₁) meets the tangent at Q(t₂) on y²=4ax, their product t₁t₂ = ______.

options.A: It depends on the specific point

questions.question: The diagram show two parabolas y²=4ax and x²=4ay. They intersect at ______.
questions.diagram_description: Two parabolas drawn on the same axes, intersecting at two points.

questions.question: Tangents at the ends of any chord of y²=4ax intersect on the ______.

options.A: Directrix (if the chord passes through focus)

questions.question: If tangent y=mx+a/m is drawn to y²=4ax, the point of tangency has x-coordinate ______.
The diagram show the parabola y²=12x. Its focus and latus rectum length are ______.
questions.diagram_description: Parabola y²=12x with focus and latus rectum marked.

questions.question: The chord PQ of y²=4ax subtends a right angle at the vertex if t₁t₂ = ______.
For the parabola y²=4ax, the slope of the normal at (at², 2at) is ______.
The diagram show the foot of the perpendicular from the focus S to the tangent at P. This foot lies on the ______.
questions.diagram_description: Focus S, tangent line at P, and the perpendicular from S to tangent with foot T.

questions.question: Three normals can be drawn from a point to y²=4ax if the point lies ______ the parabola's axis region.
The axis of the parabola (y-2)² = 8(x+3) is ______.
The diagram show the parabola y=x². Its vertex is at ______ and opens ______.
questions.diagram_description: A standard upward-opening parabola on the coordinate plane.

questions.question: The length of the sub-tangent at any point on y²=4ax equals ______.
The diagram show the normal to y²=4ax at P passing through another point Q on the parabola. The chord PQ is called a ______.
questions.diagram_description: Parabola with normal at P intersecting the curve again at Q.

questions.question: Tangent to y²=4ax at (x₁, y₁) is ______.
The directrix of y² = 12x is ______.
For y² = 4ax, the locus of the point of intersection of perpendicular tangents is the ______.
